cs:tempo:hero.title: Tempo.
cs:tempo:hero.description: An AI-driven ordering and task system designed for high-volume restaurants — built to absorb the chaos of the dinner rush and give servers their attention back.

cs:tempo:sections.heading: Overview
cs:tempo:sections.body: Tempo is an innovative and comprehensive restaurant system that integrates AI-powered ordering, payment capabilities, and a task notification/management to enhance the flow and efficiency at high-end restaurants. Imagining the Future of Restaurants With the recent advancements in AI, robotics, and emerging tech, the hospitality industry has a golden opportunity to reinvent the restaurant experience for customers and staff. Envisioning the restaurant of the future, 5–10 years out, my team and I targeted high-end restaurants to improve the flow of premium service and enhance NRC Voyix’s product offerings.

cs:tempo:sections.heading: What is high-end dining?
cs:tempo:sections.body:
High-End dining is the combination of luxurious food and exceptional service, including:
1. Small Margin of Error
2. Complex Steps of Service
3. High Expectations

cs:tempo:sections.caption: Following over 30 hours of on-site interviews and visits, along with analyzing 75+ consumer survey responses, my team and I identified 45 key insights, ultimately narrowing them down to three main findings.

cards.title: Broken Sequence of Events
cards.body: Less than 28% are willing to wait more than 28 minutes. Everyone has to work as fast as possible to avoid miscommunications — Sophia, server from Rezdora.
cards.extension: As a result of the intricate nature of services in a high-end restaurant, a single individual's inability to complete their assigned tasks or communicate effectively can potentially disrupt the sequence, leading to an extended wait time for the guests.

cards.title: Neglected Tables
cards.body: It can be inevitable for servers to make mistakes due to the amount of tables they handle. "I've been here for 2 years.. I'm at your table long before the time limit for the table greetings." — Max, Server from STK.
cards.extension: Due to unintentional neglect like understaffing as a way to maintain profit margins, a complex sequence of service, overlapping roles, or intentional neglect like staff biases towards regulars, some tables may experience sub-optimal service.

cards.title: Expectation of Perfection
cards.body:
Expectations of the Staff:
1. Taste and know everything on the menu
2. Recommend food and drinks
3. Timely Service
Server at Hilton Seelbach
cards.extension: Fine dining establishments heavily depend on reviews and reputation to attract customers. A single negative review has the potential to tarnish the restaurant's image significantly.

cs:tempo:sections.text: Across every service we studied, the breakdowns were never about the food — they were about timing and attention.
cs:tempo:sections.author: Research synthesis

cs:tempo:sections.heading: Project Objective
cs:tempo:sections.body: To use AI analysis to improve the flow and consistency of premium service at high-end restaurants.

cards.title: Eliminating the POS Terminal
cards.body: This concept takes the appearance of a conventional notepad, while incorporating voice recognition for ordering, a POS (Point of Sale) ordering system, a stylus for server modifications, and various payment options.

cards.title: Enhancing Task Communication
cards.body: This concept is designed to enhance intra-staff communication by utilizing AI for sending reminders and task assignments.

cards.title: Personalized E-ink Menu
cards.body: This concept allows users to make reservations through OpenTable, input their preferences into our database, and receive tailored suggestions on an E-Ink menu. Additionally, users have the option to provide feedback and rate the food for ongoing enhancements.

hw.name: TempoScribe
hw.body: A handheld POS system with AI speech-to-text for automatic order recording and quick edits.

hw.name: TempoTap
hw.body: A sleek payment system that allows the table to pay at their own pace.

hw.name: TempoChime
hw.body: Wearable task manager for efficient staff task distribution and coordination.

cs:tempo:sections.heading: What does TempoScribe do?
cs:tempo:sections.body: TempoScribe aims to lighten the mental workload of servers by simplifying the order-taking process. It eliminates the requirement for a POS terminal and facilitates seamless edits through a stylus, emulating the experience of using a traditional server notepad.

cs:tempo:sections.caption: Uses AI Voice Recognition to match the order taken with the seat number of the guest.

cs:tempo:sections.heading: Handwriting to Text
cs:tempo:sections.body: The integrated handwriting recognition system matches the handwritten input with the menu and identifies order specifications directly from your writing. This eliminates the need to click multiple buttons to add an order with specific details.

cs:tempo:sections.heading: Payment, with or without AI.
cs:tempo:sections.body: Guests settle the check on their own terms; the system keeps the table turning either way.

cs:tempo:sections.heading: What does TempoTap do?
cs:tempo:sections.body: Once the bill is split, the server sends the data to TempoTap. A sleek payment system that allows the table to pay at their own pace.

cs:tempo:sections.heading: What does TempoChime do?
cs:tempo:sections.body: A wearable task manager that allows for inter-staff communication. This allows servers, bussers, and food runners to have the flexibility to keep track of their tasks.

cs:tempo:sections.text: Tempo reframes the restaurant of the near future around flow — technology that recedes so hospitality can lead.
cs:tempo:sections.author: Key takeaway

cs:tempo:sections.heading: Why Tempo earns its place at the table.
cs:tempo:sections.note: based on primary research + concept projections.

items.label: of diners grow dissatisfied after 28+ min waits — Tempo targets this directly

TempoScribe, TempoTap & TempoChime replacing 3 fragmented legacy systems
